title: PD-L1 22C3 LDT on Dako automate
author: Marius MI Ilié
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iw9cfh6

steps.title: The specimens are sectioned at a...
steps.instruction: The specimens are sectioned at a thickness of 3-μm and stained on glass slides stored at 4°C within 3 days after sectioning.

steps.title: Deparaffinization, rehydration and antigen retrieval is...
steps.instruction: Deparaffinization, rehydration and antigen retrieval is performed on PT Link (Dako PT100) using the EnVision™ FLEX Target Retrieval Solution, low pH 6.0 for 53 minutes.

steps.title: Following FLEX peroxidase block for 5...
steps.instruction: Following FLEX peroxidase block for 5 minutes, specimens are incubated with primary mouse monoclonal antibody anti-PD-L1 (Ref. M365329, Dako) using a concentration of 1:50 for 60 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Specimens are then incubated with the...
steps.instruction: Specimens are then incubated with the EnVision™ FLEX+ Mouse LINKER for 30 minutes at room temperature, followed by incubation with the EnVision™ FLEX HRP visualization reagent for 30 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: The enzymatic conversion of the subsequently...
steps.instruction: The enzymatic conversion of the subsequently added 3,3’-diaminobenzidine tetrahydrochloride (DAB) chromogen followed by DAB enhancer resulted in precipitation of a visible reaction product at the site of antigen. The specimens are then counterstained with Hematoxylin and coverslipped.

steps.title: Each IHC run contained a positive...
steps.instruction: Each IHC run contained a positive control (on-slide tonsil tissue) and a negative antibody control (buffer, no primary antibody).
